title: Add MCP ToolAnnotations (readOnly/destructive hints) to every wg-vpn tool
why_we_need_this:
The read-only-vs-mutating status of each wg-vpn MCP tool currently lives only in docs (mcp/docs/tool-surface.md, confirmation-gates.md) and in the confirmation-gate code. MCP ToolAnnotations surface that fact in the tool metadata the host reads at list time. The Go SDK already in use (modelcontextprotocol go-sdk version 1.6.1) supports it: the mcp.Tool struct has an Annotations pointer field of type ToolAnnotations (defined in the SDK's mcp/protocol.go) carrying ReadOnlyHint, DestructiveHint, IdempotentHint, OpenWorldHint and Title. Annotating the 20 tools lets any MCP host machine-reason about the surface (guard the destructive verb, skip confirmation UX on read-only tools, present a cleaner catalog) without parsing our docs. Wrapper-only, no behavior change, no dashboard or terraform impact. This came from comparing wg-vpn's MCP to the Praxis MCP, which annotates its tools while wg-vpn does not.</why_we_need_this>
<parameter name="acceptance_criteria">Add a ToolAnnotations block to every tool registration in the mcp/internal/tools package (read-only tools in tools.go, mutating tools in mutating.go, get_host_metrics in host_metrics.go). Map the hints as follows. The 14 read-only tools — get_metrics, get_system_metrics, get_traffic_metrics, get_client_metrics, list_clients, get_client_config, get_client_history, get_service_status, get_server_info, get_alerts, get_snapshot, get_geo, get_health, get_host_metrics — get ReadOnlyHint true. preview_delete_client also gets ReadOnlyHint true, because it only previews and issues a token and mutates nothing. delete_client gets DestructiveHint true and ReadOnlyHint false, as the sole irreversible verb. add_client, edit_client, enable_client and disable_client are reversible mutations: ReadOnlyHint false and DestructiveHint false, with IdempotentHint set only where it genuinely applies (for example enable and disable), never on add_client. Leave OpenWorldHint unset or false, since all tools reach only the closed dashboard API over the tunnel; justify that in a code comment. Verify the exact ToolAnnotations field names and types against the vendored SDK source (the go-sdk protocol.go in the local module cache) before writing — no guessing. This is additive metadata only: do NOT change any tool behavior, arguments, return shape, or confirmation-gate logic. Reuse the existing addNoArgTool, addRangeTool and get helpers; if threading a hints parameter through those helpers is cleaner than duplicating at each call site, do that, keeping the change DRY, minimal and readable. Record the rejected sibling idea inline (in tool-surface.md or the route README): a broad re-model-every-return-into-typed-structs change was considered and rejected, because the dashboard JSON API endpoints already return JSON so the passthrough is already structured, re-typing would couple the MCP to the dashboard schema and break the deliberate byte-faithful-wrapper invariant, and get_host_metrics is the sole justified exception because the metrics endpoint returns text not JSON. Update docs to note that each tool now carries ToolAnnotations and which class of tool gets which hint; the tool count is unchanged at 20 total, 14 read-only, 6 mutating. Add or extend a unit test asserting a sample of tools expose the expected annotations (get_health has ReadOnlyHint true, delete_client has DestructiveHint true) via the in-memory MCP client and server harness already used in tools_test.go and mutating_test.go. From the mcp directory, run the build, vet and test commands for all packages and confirm they pass. Then run update_project_context for the mcp-server route.
acceptance_criteria: Add a ToolAnnotations block to every tool registration in the mcp/internal/tools package (read-only tools in tools.go, mutating tools in mutating.go, get_host_metrics in host_metrics.go). important_constraints: MCP module only (the mcp directory). Do NOT modify the dashboard or terraform code or any dashboard API handler. No new dependencies — the go-sdk version 1.6.1 already in go.mod provides ToolAnnotations; do not bump the SDK. Additive metadata only: zero change to tool behavior, arguments, return bytes, or gate logic. Verify SDK field names against the vendored module source before coding. Taking effect on an installed binary needs a new mcp release tag and reinstall — call that out but do NOT cut the release as part of this task.
